name: Nellie Lutcher
abstract: Nellie Rose Lutcher (October 15, 1912 – June 8, 2007) was an American R&B and jazz singer and pianist, who gained prominence in the late 1940s and early 1950s. Lutcher was most recognizable for her diction and exaggerated pronunciation and was credited as an influence by Nina Simone among others.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Jazz Cabaret with Cab Calloway

about_quotes.speaker: Billie Pierce and De De Pierce
about_quotes.evidence: Nellie Lutcher joined my band then. She was the pianist in our band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Billie Pierce and De De Pierce Interview, 1959-04-02

about_quotes.speaker: Leonidas Raymond Young
about_quotes.evidence: I had done a lot of recording with different vocalists -- because you know, like "Hurry on Down" and "Brown Frame" and all of those things, I did with Nellie Lutcher.

about_quotes.speaker: Emile Barnes and Paul Barnes
about_quotes.evidence: Nellie Lutcher was on the piano... Paul Barnes on saxophone... that was in 1932.
about_quotes.doc_title: Emile Barnes and Paul Barnes Interview, 1959-10-01

about_quotes.speaker: Lionel Reason
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Well, did you and Nelly Lutcher ever play two pianos together? Yeah. >> [INTERVIEWEE] It was two pianos. See, what they had, a lot of places where, it wasn't a long, long time in Gay from Ingrace. But at Sharon's at the Beach there were two pianos. So Nelly was playing one side to anot

about_quotes.speaker: Helen Humes
about_quotes.evidence: Nellie Lutcher, bless her heart, she came and she said, 'Helen I'll stay right here with you until you go on'. She said, 'don't be scared'. And I said, 'well you know every time I get nervous and I get hoarse and everything'. She ran across the street to the drug store and she brought me more cough

about_quotes.speaker: Billie Pierce and De De Pierce
